message:
Rename tenzir-changelog to tenzir-ship

Adapt documentation to the renamed CLI tool and unified `show` command:

- Update all references from tenzir-changelog to tenzir-ship
- Adapt to unified `show` command (replacing `show entries`/`show releases`)
- Document scope tokens for the `show` command
- Update changelog sync script and project configuration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -41,7 +41,7 @@ Let's discuss each component in detail.
 ### `changelog`: User-facing changes
 
 The `changelog` directory tracks user-facing changes to the package using the
-[Changelog Framework](/reference/changelog-framework). It contains unreleased
+[Ship Framework](/reference/ship-framework). It contains unreleased
 entries and versioned releases that document breaking changes, new features, bug
 fixes, and other modifications. Each entry includes metadata such as authors,
 pull request numbers, and creation dates. See the guide for [maintaining a

@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@ title: Maintain a changelog
 ---
 
 This guide shows you how to use
-[`tenzir-changelog`](/reference/changelog-framework) to manage changelog
+[`tenzir-ship`](/reference/ship-framework) to manage changelog
 entries, create releases, and publish release notes. You'll learn the complete
 workflow from adding your first entry to publishing a release on GitHub.
 
@@ -18,7 +18,7 @@ workflow from adding your first entry to publishing a release on GitHub.
 Create your first changelog entry to scaffold the project:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog add
+uvx tenzir-ship add
 ```
 
 The wizard will guide you through the process of creating a new changelog entry.
@@ -36,20 +36,20 @@ extra files.
 View the current changelog for your working tree:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog show
+uvx tenzir-ship show
 ```
 
 The default table lists every entry with row numbers, making it easy to
 reference specific items. Row numbers count backward from the newest entry, so
 `#1` always targets the latest change.
 
-Inspect a card layout with `uvx tenzir-changelog show -c 1` or export a release
-via `uvx tenzir-changelog release notes v0.2.0`.
+Inspect a card layout with `uvx tenzir-ship show --card 1` or export release
+notes with `uvx tenzir-ship show v0.2.0 --release --markdown`.
 
 Add entries as you prepare pull requests:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog add \
+uvx tenzir-ship add \
   --title "Introduce pipeline builder" \
   --type feature \
   --pr 101
@@ -63,10 +63,10 @@ auto-detection.
 
 ### Add entries for pull requests
 
-Run `uvx tenzir-changelog add` while preparing pull requests to capture changes:
+Run `uvx tenzir-ship add` while preparing pull requests to capture changes:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog add \
+uvx tenzir-ship add \
   --title "Fix authentication timeout" \
   --type bugfix \
   --description "Resolves session expiry issue." \
@@ -82,7 +82,7 @@ Use `--co-author` to credit additional contributors alongside the automatically
 inferred author:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog add \
+uvx tenzir-ship add \
   --title "Implement caching layer" \
   --type feature \
   --co-author claude
@@ -93,7 +93,7 @@ contributions. The CLI infers the primary author from environment variables or
 the GitHub CLI, then appends co-authors in the order you specify:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog add \
+uvx tenzir-ship add \
   --title "Refactor authentication" \
   --type change \
   --co-author claude \
@@ -105,7 +105,7 @@ You can also combine `--author` with `--co-author` to set the primary author
 explicitly:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog add \
+uvx tenzir-ship add \
   --title "Fix race condition" \
   --type bugfix \

@@ -117,19 +117,19 @@ uvx tenzir-changelog add \
 List all unreleased entries:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog show
+uvx tenzir-ship show
 ```
 
 Show detailed information for a specific entry:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog show -c 1
+uvx tenzir-ship show --card 1
 ```
 
 The card view displays detailed metadata and the full entry body:
 
 ```text
-❯ tenzir-changelog show -c 1
+❯ tenzir-ship show --card 1
 ╭─ 🐞 Normalize release note paragraphs ───────────────────────────────────╮
 │ Entry ID:  normalize-release-note-paragraphs                             │
 │ Type:      bugfix                                                        │
@@ -145,35 +145,53 @@ The card view displays detailed metadata and the full entry body:
 Filter by project when working with multiple projects:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog show --project core
+uvx tenzir-ship show --project core
 ```
 
 ### Validate changelog structure
 
 Run validation checks to catch issues early:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog validate
+uvx tenzir-ship validate
 ```
 
 The validator reports missing metadata, unused entries, duplicate entry IDs, and
 configuration drift. Add this to CI pipelines to enforce metadata completeness.
 
+### View project statistics
+
+Get an overview of your project's release history and entry distribution:
+
+```sh
+uvx tenzir-ship stats
+```
+
+Single projects show a vertical card layout with sections for project metadata,
+releases, entry types, and status. Multi-module projects display a compact table
+comparing all modules.
+
+Export statistics as JSON for automation:
+
+```sh
+uvx tenzir-ship stats --json
+```
+
 ## Release workflow
 
 ### Create a release
 
 Preview the release before committing changes:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release create v5.4.0
+uvx tenzir-ship release create v5.4.0
 ```
 
 The command shows which entries will be included and performs a dry run. When
 the summary looks good, re-run with `--yes` to commit the changes:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release create v5.4.0 --yes
+uvx tenzir-ship release create v5.4.0 --yes
 ```
 
 This moves entries from `unreleased/` to `releases/v5.4.0/entries/`, generates
@@ -182,23 +200,23 @@ This moves entries from `unreleased/` to `releases/v5.4.0/entries/`, generates
 Use version bump shortcuts instead of typing the full version:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release create --patch --yes
-uvx tenzir-changelog release create --minor --yes
-uvx tenzir-changelog release create --major --yes
+uvx tenzir-ship release create --patch --yes
+uvx tenzir-ship release create --minor --yes
+uvx tenzir-ship release create --major --yes
 ```
 
 Add an inline intro summary:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release create v5.4.0 \
+uvx tenzir-ship release create v5.4.0 \
   --intro "Major stability improvements." \
   --yes
 ```
 
 Alternatively, provide intro content from a file:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release create v5.4.0 \
+uvx tenzir-ship release create v5.4.0 \
   --intro-file intro.md \
   --yes
 ```
@@ -208,49 +226,58 @@ command embeds its content in `manifest.yaml` and includes it in `notes.md`.
 
 ### Export release notes
 
-Re-export release notes without modifying files:
+Export release notes for a specific version:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release notes v5.4.0
+uvx tenzir-ship show v5.4.0 --release --markdown
 ```
 
 Export as JSON for programmatic use:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release notes v5.4.0 -j
+uvx tenzir-ship show v5.4.0 --release --json
 ```
 
 Use compact format for bullet-list layout:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release notes v5.4.0 --compact
+uvx tenzir-ship show v5.4.0 --release --compact --markdown
 ```
 
 Preview unreleased entries as if they were already released:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release notes unreleased
+uvx tenzir-ship show --release --markdown
 ```
 
+Export all releases in a single invocation for batch processing:
+
+```sh
+uvx tenzir-ship show all --release --json
+```
+
+This exports every release as a JSON array, which is useful for documentation
+sync scripts that need all release data at once.
+
 ### Publish to GitHub
 
 Publish directly from the changelog project using the CLI:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release publish v5.4.0 --yes
+uvx tenzir-ship release publish v5.4.0 --yes
 ```
 
 Include tagging and pushing via the CLI:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release publish v5.4.0 --tag --yes
+uvx tenzir-ship release publish v5.4.0 --tag --yes
 ```
 
 Draft or prerelease variants:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release publish v5.4.0 --draft --yes
-uvx tenzir-changelog release publish v5.4.0 --prerelease --yes
+uvx tenzir-ship release publish v5.4.0 --draft --yes
+uvx tenzir-ship release publish v5.4.0 --prerelease --yes
 ```
 
 :::note[Terminology]

 ### Prepare release notes

 ```

 Verify everything is correct:

 A clean run prints `All changelog files look good!`. You can remove `intro.md`
@@ -421,18 +448,18 @@ now that its content is embedded in the release file.
 Print the release notes:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release notes v0.1.0
+uvx tenzir-ship show v0.1.0 --release --markdown
 ```
 
 Export as JSON for programmatic use:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog release notes v0.1.0 -j
+uvx tenzir-ship show v0.1.0 --release --json
 ```
 
 ## Tips and best practices
 
-- **Automate validation**: Add `uvx tenzir-changelog validate` to CI pipelines
+- **Automate validation**: Add `uvx tenzir-ship validate` to CI pipelines
   to enforce metadata completeness before merging pull requests.
 - **Use PR numbers**: Always include `--pr` when adding entries to link changes
   back to pull requests for traceability.
@@ -447,18 +474,18 @@ uvx tenzir-changelog release notes v0.1.0 -j
 
 ## Troubleshooting
 
-- **Validation failures**: Run `uvx tenzir-changelog validate` to identify
+- **Validation failures**: Run `uvx tenzir-ship validate` to identify
   specific issues. Common problems include missing required fields, duplicate
   entry IDs, or unused entry files.
 - **Component mismatch**: If you configured `components`, ensure entries either
   omit the `component` field or use one of the allowed labels.
 - **Configuration not found**: Ensure `config.yaml` exists in the changelog root
   or `package.yaml` sits next to the `changelog/` directory. Run
-  `uvx tenzir-changelog add` once to scaffold the configuration.
+  `uvx tenzir-ship add` once to scaffold the configuration.
 - **Version bump fails**: Bump flags read the most recent release manifest on
   disk. Create an initial release with an explicit version before using
   `--patch/--minor/--major`.
 
 ## Further reading
 
-- [Changelog framework reference](/reference/changelog-framework)
+- [Ship framework reference](/reference/ship-framework)

@@ -1,22 +1,22 @@
 ---
-title: Changelog Framework
+title: Ship Framework
 ---
 
-The [`tenzir-changelog`](https://github.com/tenzir/changelog) harness curates
-changelog entries, ships release notes, and assembles the public changelog
-across Tenzir repositories. Use this page as a reference for concepts,
+[`tenzir-ship`](https://github.com/tenzir/ship) helps you ship faster with
+automated release engineering. Manage changelogs, generate release notes, and
+publish GitHub releases. Use this page as a reference for concepts,
 configuration, and CLI details. For step-by-step walkthroughs, see the guide for
 [maintaining a changelog](/guides/package-management/maintain-a-changelog).
 
 ## Install
 
-`tenzir-changelog` ships as a Python package that requires Python 3.12 or later.
+`tenzir-ship` ships as a Python package that requires Python 3.12 or later.
 Install it with [`uv`](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) (or `pip`) and verify the
 console script:
 
 ```sh
-uv add tenzir-changelog
-uvx tenzir-changelog --help
+uv add tenzir-ship
+uvx tenzir-ship --help
 ```
 
 ## Python API
@@ -26,7 +26,7 @@ Drive the CLI flows from another Python process by importing the
 
 ```python
 from pathlib import Path
-from tenzir_changelog import Changelog
+from tenzir_ship import Changelog
 
 client = Changelog(root=Path("changelog"))
 client.add(
@@ -35,8 +35,11 @@ client.add(
     description="Body text",
     co_authors=["claude"],
 )
+
+# Show entries using scope tokens as identifiers
 client.show(identifiers=["unreleased"], view="markdown", explicit_links=True)
-client.release_notes("v1.0.0", explicit_links=True)
+client.show(identifiers=["v1.0.0"], release_mode=True, view="markdown")
+client.show(identifiers=["released"], release_mode=True, view="json")
 
 # Get the latest version
 version = client.release_version()  # Returns "v1.0.0"
@@ -46,8 +49,8 @@ bare_version = client.release_version(bare=True)  # Returns "1.0.0"
 client.release_publish(create_tag=True, assume_yes=True)
 ```
 
-For advanced scenarios, reuse `tenzir_changelog.create_cli_context` and call
-the helper functions from `tenzir_changelog.cli` directly.
+For advanced scenarios, reuse `tenzir_ship.create_cli_context` and call
+the helper functions from `tenzir_ship.cli` directly.
 
 ## Core concepts
 
@@ -119,7 +122,7 @@ structure may look like:
 Run changelog commands from the project root or the `changelog/` directory:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-changelog [command] [options]
+uvx tenzir-ship [command] [options]
 ```
 
 All commands accept `--config` to point at an explicit configuration file (YAML
@@ -136,33 +139,87 @@ root or the `changelog/` directory without repeating `--root`.
 Display changelog entries in multiple views.
 
 ```text
-tenzir-changelog show [identifiers...] [options]
+tenzir-ship show [identifiers...] [options]
 ```
 
-| Option                | Description                                                 |
-| --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
-| `identifiers`         | Row numbers, entry IDs, release versions, or `-` (optional) |
-| `-c/--card`           | Show detailed cards for matching entries                    |
-| `-m/--markdown`       | Export as Markdown                                          |
-| `-j/--json`           | Export as JSON                                              |
-| `--compact`           | Use compact bullet-list layout                              |
-| `--no-compact`        | Use detailed card layout                                    |
-| `--no-emoji`          | Remove type emoji from output                               |
-| `--explicit-links`    | Render @mentions and PR refs as explicit Markdown links     |
-| `--project <id>`      | Filter to specific project (repeatable)                     |
-| `--component <label>` | Filter to specific component (repeatable)                   |
-| `--banner`            | Display project banner above table output                   |
+| Option                | Description                                             |
+| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `identifiers`         | Row numbers, entry IDs, scope tokens, or versions       |
+| `-c/--card`           | Show detailed cards for matching entries                |
+| `-m/--markdown`       | Export as Markdown                                      |
+| `-j/--json`           | Export as JSON                                          |
+| `--release`           | Display entries grouped by release with full metadata   |
+| `--compact`           | Use compact bullet-list layout                          |
+| `--no-compact`        | Use detailed card layout                                |
+| `--no-emoji`          | Remove type emoji from output                           |
+| `--explicit-links`    | Render @mentions and PR refs as explicit Markdown links |
+| `--project <id>`      | Filter to specific project (repeatable)                 |
+| `--component <label>` | Filter to specific component (repeatable)               |
+| `--banner`            | Display project banner above table output               |
+
+#### Scope tokens
+
+Scope tokens control which entries to display. Pass one as a positional
+argument:
+
+| Token        | Description                                         |
+| ------------ | --------------------------------------------------- |
+| `all`        | All entries (released and unreleased) — the default |
+| `unreleased` | Only unreleased entries                             |
+| `released`   | Only released entries                               |
+| `latest`     | Only entries from the latest release                |
+
+You can combine a scope token with version identifiers (except `all`, which
+must be used alone):
+
+```sh
+# Show a specific release
+tenzir-ship show v1.0.0
+
+# Show only unreleased entries
+tenzir-ship show unreleased
+
+# Show released entries, filtering to specific versions
+tenzir-ship show released v1.0.0 v1.1.0
+```
 
 The table view (default) lists entries with ID, title, type, project, PRs, and
 authors. Row numbers count backward from the newest entry, so `#1` always
 targets the latest change.
 
+Use `--release` to display entries grouped by release with full release
+metadata. This is the recommended mode for exporting release notes:
+
+```sh
+# Export a specific release as Markdown
+tenzir-ship show v1.0.0 --release --markdown
+
+# Preview unreleased entries as release notes
+tenzir-ship show unreleased --release --markdown
+
+# Export all releases as JSON
+tenzir-ship show all --release --json
+
+# Export only released versions (exclude unreleased)
+tenzir-ship show released --release --json
+
+# Show entries from the latest release only
+tenzir-ship show latest --release --markdown
+```
+
+Use `--explicit-links` when rendering Markdown outside of GitHub. By default,
+the output uses `@username` and `#123` references that GitHub auto-links. With
+`--explicit-links`, these become explicit Markdown links like
+`[@username](https://github.com/username)` and
+`[#123](https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123)`, making the output portable to
+documentation sites, blogs, or other Markdown renderers.
+
 ### add
 
 Create a new changelog entry in `unreleased/`.
 
 ```text
-tenzir-changelog add [options]
+tenzir-ship add [options]
 ```
 
 | Option                 | Description                                  |
@@ -195,7 +252,7 @@ contributions. Duplicates are removed automatically while preserving order.
 Stage a release under `releases/<version>/`.
 
 ```text
-tenzir-changelog release create [version] [options]
+tenzir-ship release create [version] [options]
 ```
 
 | Option                | Description                                                |
@@ -221,7 +278,7 @@ exists, the CLI appends additional unreleased entries.
 Print the latest released version.
 
 ```text
-tenzir-changelog release version [options]
+tenzir-ship release version [options]
 ```
 
 | Option   | Description                      |
@@ -233,47 +290,23 @@ output from other commands:
 
 ```sh
 # Get the latest version
-tenzir-changelog release version
+tenzir-ship release version
 # Output: v1.2.0
 
 # Get version without prefix (for semver tools)
-tenzir-changelog release version --bare
+tenzir-ship release version --bare
 # Output: 1.2.0
 
 # Use in commit messages
-git commit -m "Release $(tenzir-changelog release version)"
-```
-
-### release notes
-
-Re-export release notes without modifying files.
-
-```text
-tenzir-changelog release notes <identifier> [options]
+git commit -m "Release $(tenzir-ship release version)"
 ```
 
-| Option             | Description                                             |
-| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- |
-| `identifier`       | Release version or `-` for unreleased                   |
-| `-m`               | Export as Markdown (default)                            |
-| `-j`               | Export as JSON                                          |
-| `--compact`        | Use bullet-list layout                                  |
-| `--no-emoji`       | Drop type icons                                         |
-| `--explicit-links` | Render @mentions and PR refs as explicit Markdown links |
-
-Use `--explicit-links` when rendering Markdown outside of GitHub. By default,
-the output uses `@username` and `#123` references that GitHub auto-links. With
-`--explicit-links`, these become explicit Markdown links like
-`[@username](https://github.com/username)` and
-`[#123](https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123)`, making the output portable to
-documentation sites, blogs, or other Markdown renderers.
-
 ### release publish
 
 Publish a release to GitHub via `gh`.
 
 ```text
-tenzir-changelog release publish [version] [options]
+tenzir-ship release publish [version] [options]
 ```
 
 | Option             | Description                                             |
@@ -342,27 +375,27 @@ A full release workflow with commit and tag:
 
 ```sh
 # 1. Create the release (generates manifest.yaml and notes.md)
-tenzir-changelog release create --minor --yes
+tenzir-ship release create --minor --yes
 
 # 2. Review the generated notes
-tenzir-changelog release notes
+tenzir-ship show --release --markdown
 
 # 3. Stage release files
 git add changelog/releases/
 
 # 4. Publish with commit and tag (defaults to latest release)
-tenzir-changelog release publish --commit --tag --yes
+tenzir-ship release publish --commit --tag --yes
 ```
 
 Alternatively, commit manually to customize the message using `release version`:
 
 ```sh
 # 3. Stage and commit with custom message
 git add changelog/releases/
-git commit -m "Release $(tenzir-changelog release version)"
+git commit -m "Release $(tenzir-ship release version)"
 
 # 4. Publish (defaults to latest release)
-tenzir-changelog release publish --tag --yes
+tenzir-ship release publish --tag --yes
 ```
 
 Both workflows are version-agnostic after step 1. The `release publish` command
@@ -374,24 +407,54 @@ Run structural checks across entry files, release manifests, and exported
 documentation.
 
 ```text
-tenzir-changelog validate
+tenzir-ship validate
 ```
 
 The validator reports missing metadata, unused entries, duplicate entry IDs, and
 configuration drift across repositories. When modules are configured, validation
 runs against the parent project and all discovered modules. Issues from modules
 are prefixed with the module ID.
 
-### modules
+### stats
 
-List discovered modules when a `modules` glob pattern is configured.
+Display project and release statistics.
 
 ```text
-tenzir-changelog modules
+tenzir-ship stats [options]
+```
+
+| Option    | Description                           |
+| --------- | ------------------------------------- |
+| `--table` | Force table view even for one project |
+| `--json`  | Export statistics as JSON             |
+
+The command adapts its display based on the project structure:
+
+- **Single projects**: Vertical card layout with detailed sections for project
+  metadata, releases, entry types, and status
+- **Multi-module projects**: Compact table comparing all modules side by side
+
+Statistics include:
+
+- **Project info**: Name, latest version, age since last release
+- **Releases**: Total count, time span, release cadence (exponentially weighted
+  for recent activity)
+- **Entry types**: Distribution of breaking, feature, change, and bugfix entries
+  with percentages
+- **Entry status**: Total, shipped, and unreleased counts
+
+The release cadence uses exponential weighting to emphasize recent activity. A
+project releasing monthly for the past quarter shows higher cadence than one
+with sporadic releases.
+
+Use `--json` to export structured data for automation:
+
+```sh
+tenzir-ship stats --json
 ```
 
-The command displays a table with module ID, name, relative path, and count of
-unreleased entries. Use this to discover module paths for `--root` operations.
+The JSON output includes the project root path, parent project statistics, and
+an array of module statistics when modules are configured.
 
 ## Configuration
 
@@ -444,7 +507,7 @@ ignores any `--pr`, `--author`, or `--co-author` flags (with a warning). Use
 these options for projects that don't use GitHub pull requests or prefer
 anonymous changelog entries.
 
-The first invocation of `tenzir-changelog add` scaffolds a `changelog/`
+The first invocation of `tenzir-ship add` scaffolds a `changelog/`
 subdirectory with `config.yaml`, inferring defaults from the parent directory
 name. When you provide an explicit `--root` flag, the CLI uses that directory
 directly. Projects with `package.yaml` next to `changelog/` reuse the package
@@ -629,10 +692,10 @@ each entry belongs to:
 
 ```sh
 # Show all entries (parent + modules)
-tenzir-changelog show
+tenzir-ship show
 
 # Show only parent entries
-tenzir-changelog show --project parent
+tenzir-ship show --project parent
 ```
 
 ### Aggregated release summaries
@@ -662,7 +725,7 @@ The release manifest records each module's version at release time in the
 include entries from module releases newer than the recorded version. This
 prevents duplicate entries across parent releases.
 
-The `release notes` command also renders module summaries when viewing a
+The `show --release` command also renders module summaries when viewing a
 specific release, using the same incremental logic based on the manifest's
 recorded module versions.
 
@@ -673,14 +736,14 @@ specific module:
 
 ```sh
 # Add entry to a module
-tenzir-changelog --root ../packages/amazon_vpc_flow/changelog add --title "Add parser"
